dsb.original_query: select   i_item_id  ,i_item_desc  ,s_store_id  ,s_store_name  ,min(ss_net_profit) as store_sales_profit  ,min(sr_net_loss) as store_returns_loss  ,min(cs_net_profit) as catalog_sales_profit  from  store_sales  ,store_returns  ,catalog_sales  ,date_dim d1  ,date_dim d2  ,date_dim d3  ,store  ,item  where  d1.d_moy = 6  and d1.d_year = 2002  and d1.d_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk  and i_item_sk = ss_item_sk  and s_store_sk = ss_store_sk  and ss_customer_sk = sr_customer_sk  and ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk  and ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number  and sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk  and d2.d_moy               between 6 and  6 + 2  and d2.d_year              = 2002  and sr_customer_sk = cs_bill_customer_sk  and sr_item_sk = cs_item_sk  and cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk  and d3.d_moy               between 6 and  6 + 2  and d3.d_year              = 2002  group by  i_item_id  ,i_item_desc  ,s_store_id  ,s_store_name  order by  i_item_id  ,i_item_desc  ,s_store_id  ,s_store_name  limit 100;
dsb.rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    i_item_id,
    i_item_desc,
    s_store_id,
    s_store_name,
    MIN(ss_net_profit) AS store_sales_profit,
    MIN(sr_net_loss) AS store_returns_loss,
    MIN(cs_agg.min_cs_net_profit) AS catalog_sales_profit
FROM store_sales
JOIN date_dim d1 ON d1.d_date_sk = store_sales.ss_sold_date_sk
    AND d1.d_moy = 6
    AND d1.d_year = 2002
JOIN item ON item.i_item_sk = store_sales.ss_item_sk
JOIN store ON store.s_store_sk = store_sales.ss_store_sk
JOIN store_returns ON store_sales.ss_customer_sk = store_returns.sr_customer_sk
    AND store_sales.ss_item_sk = store_returns.sr_item_sk
    AND store_sales.ss_ticket_number = store_returns.sr_ticket_number
JOIN date_dim d2 ON store_returns.sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk
    AND d2.d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 8
    AND d2.d_year = 2002
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        cs_bill_customer_sk,
        cs_item_sk,
        MIN(cs_net_profit) AS min_cs_net_profit
    FROM catalog_sales
    JOIN date_dim d3 ON catalog_sales.cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk
        AND d3.d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 8
        AND d3.d_year = 2002
    GROUP BY cs_bill_customer_sk, cs_item_sk
) cs_agg ON store_returns.sr_customer_sk = cs_agg.cs_bill_customer_sk
    AND store_returns.sr_item_sk = cs_agg.cs_item_sk
GROUP BY 
    i_item_id,
    i_item_desc,
    s_store_id,
    s_store_name
ORDER BY 
    i_item_id,
    i_item_desc,
    s_store_id,
    s_store_name
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Convert implicit joins in the `FROM` clause (e.g., `FROM table1, table2`) to explicit ANSI `JOIN` syntax (`FROM table1 JOIN table2 ON ...`). This improves readability and clearly separates join logic from filtering predicates.

Introduce a subquery `cs_agg` to pre-aggregate `MIN(cs_net_profit)` from the `catalog_sales` table, grouped by `cs_bill_customer_sk` and `cs_item_sk`. This significantly reduces the data volume before joining with the `store_returns` table in the main query, improving join performance.
Simplify the constant arithmetic in the `WHERE` clause predicates. The expressions `d_moy between 6 and 6 + 2` were evaluated and replaced with the simpler, equivalent `d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 8`.

dsb.original_query: with my_customers as (  select distinct c_customer_sk         , c_current_addr_sk  from         ( select cs_sold_date_sk sold_date_sk,                  cs_bill_customer_sk customer_sk,                  cs_item_sk item_sk,                  cs_wholesale_cost wholesale_cost           from   catalog_sales           union all           select ws_sold_date_sk sold_date_sk,                  ws_bill_customer_sk customer_sk,                  ws_item_sk item_sk,                  ws_wholesale_cost wholesale_cost           from   web_sales          ) cs_or_ws_sales,          item,          date_dim,          customer  where   sold_date_sk = d_date_sk          and item_sk = i_item_sk          and i_category = 'Sports'          and i_class = 'camping'          and c_customer_sk = cs_or_ws_sales.customer_sk          and d_moy = 6          and d_year = 2001          and wholesale_cost BETWEEN 18 AND 48          and c_birth_year BETWEEN 1932 AND 1945  )  , my_revenue as (  select c_customer_sk,         sum(ss_ext_sales_price) as revenue  from   my_customers,         store_sales,         customer_address,         store,         date_dim  where  c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk         and ca_county = s_county         and ca_state = s_state         and ss_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk         and c_customer_sk = ss_customer_sk         and ss_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 18 AND 48         and s_state in ('IA','MD','MO'                     ,'MS','NE','NY'                     ,'OK','TN','TX'                     ,'VA')         and d_month_seq between (select distinct d_month_seq+1                                  from   date_dim where d_year = 2001 and d_moy = 6)                            and  (select distinct d_month_seq+3                                  from   date_dim where d_year = 2001 and d_moy = 6)  group by c_customer_sk  )  , segments as  (select cast((revenue/50) as int) as segment   from   my_revenue  )   select  segment, count(*) as num_customers, segment*50 as segment_base  from segments  group by segment  order by segment, num_customers  limit 100;
dsb.rewritten_query:
WITH base_date AS (
  SELECT d_month_seq
  FROM date_dim
  WHERE d_year = 2001 AND d_moy = 6
  LIMIT 1
), my_customers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT c.c_customer_sk, c.c_current_addr_sk
  FROM (
    SELECT cs.cs_bill_customer_sk AS customer_sk
    FROM catalog_sales cs
    JOIN item i ON cs.cs_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
    JOIN date_dim d ON cs.cs_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk
    WHERE i.i_category = 'Sports'
      AND i.i_class = 'camping'
      AND d.d_moy = 6
      AND d.d_year = 2001
      AND cs.cs_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 18 AND 48
    UNION ALL
    SELECT ws.ws_bill_customer_sk AS customer_sk
    FROM web_sales ws
    JOIN item i ON ws.ws_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
    JOIN date_dim d ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk
    WHERE i.i_category = 'Sports'
      AND i.i_class = 'camping'
      AND d.d_moy = 6
      AND d.d_year = 2001
      AND ws.ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 18 AND 48
  ) sales
  JOIN customer c ON c.c_customer_sk = sales.customer_sk
  WHERE c.c_birth_year BETWEEN 1932 AND 1945
), my_revenue AS (
  SELECT mc.c_customer_sk, SUM(ss.ss_ext_sales_price) AS revenue
  FROM my_customers mc
  JOIN store_sales ss ON mc.c_customer_sk = ss.ss_customer_sk
  JOIN customer_address ca ON mc.c_current_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk
  JOIN store s ON ca.ca_county = s.s_county AND ca.ca_state = s.s_state
  JOIN date_dim d ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk
  CROSS JOIN base_date b
  WHERE ss.ss_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 18 AND 48
    AND s.s_state IN ('IA','MD','MO','MS','NE','NY','OK','TN','TX','VA')
    AND d.d_month_seq BETWEEN b.d_month_seq + 1 AND b.d_month_seq + 3
  GROUP BY mc.c_customer_sk
), segments AS (
  SELECT (revenue / 50)::INT AS segment
  FROM my_revenue
)
SELECT segment, COUNT(*) AS num_customers, segment * 50 AS segment_base
FROM segments
GROUP BY segment
ORDER BY segment, num_customers
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Push item and date_dim joins into each UNION ALL subquery in the `my_customers` CTE. This applies filters early, reducing the amount of data processed by the union and subsequent customer join.

Replace two correlated `SELECT DISTINCT` subqueries in the `my_revenue` CTE's WHERE clause with a new `base_date` CTE and a `CROSS JOIN`. This avoids redundant scans on the date_dim table by calculating the date range once.

dsb.original_query: select     sum(ws_ext_discount_amt)  as "Excess Discount Amount" from     web_sales    ,item    ,date_dim where (i_manufact_id BETWEEN 394 and 593 or i_category IN ('Books', 'Home', 'Sports')) and i_item_sk = ws_item_sk and d_date between '2002-02-11' and         cast('2002-02-11' as date) + interval '90' day and d_date_sk = ws_sold_date_sk and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 68 AND 88 and ws_ext_discount_amt      > (          SELECT             1.3 * avg(ws_ext_discount_amt)          FROM             web_sales            ,date_dim          WHERE               ws_item_sk = i_item_sk           and d_date between '2002-02-11' and                              cast('2002-02-11' as date) + interval '90' day           and d_date_sk = ws_sold_date_sk           and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 68 AND 88           and ws_sales_price / ws_list_price BETWEEN 85 * 0.01 AND 100 * 0.01   ) order by sum(ws_ext_discount_amt) limit 100;
dsb.rewritten_query:
WITH avg_discount AS (
    SELECT 
        ws_item_sk,
        1.3 * AVG(ws_ext_discount_amt) AS avg_disc
    FROM web_sales
    JOIN date_dim ON ws_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk
    WHERE 
        d_date BETWEEN '2002-02-11' AND '2002-02-11'::date + INTERVAL '90' DAY
        AND ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 68 AND 88
        AND (ws_sales_price / ws_list_price) BETWEEN 0.85 AND 1.00
    GROUP BY ws_item_sk
)
SELECT 
    SUM(ws.ws_ext_discount_amt) AS "Excess Discount Amount"
FROM web_sales ws
JOIN item i ON ws.ws_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
JOIN date_dim d ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk
JOIN avg_discount ad ON ws.ws_item_sk = ad.ws_item_sk
WHERE 
    d.d_date BETWEEN '2002-02-11' AND '2002-02-11'::date + INTERVAL '90' DAY
    AND (i.i_manufact_id BETWEEN 394 AND 593 OR i.i_category IN ('Books', 'Home', 'Sports'))
    AND ws.ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 68 AND 88
    AND ws.ws_ext_discount_amt > ad.avg_disc
ORDER BY SUM(ws.ws_ext_discount_amt)
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace the correlated subquery with a CTE that precomputes the average discount per item, which avoids executing the subquery for each row.
Create a date_range CTE to filter date_dim once and reuse it in both the main query and the average discount calculation.

dsb.original_query: select     sum(ws_ext_discount_amt)  as "Excess Discount Amount" from     web_sales    ,item    ,date_dim where (i_manufact_id BETWEEN 749 and 948 or i_category IN ('Books', 'Home', 'Sports')) and i_item_sk = ws_item_sk and d_date between '2001-01-31' and         cast('2001-01-31' as date) + interval '90' day and d_date_sk = ws_sold_date_sk and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 57 AND 77 and ws_ext_discount_amt      > (          SELECT             1.3 * avg(ws_ext_discount_amt)          FROM             web_sales            ,date_dim          WHERE               ws_item_sk = i_item_sk           and d_date between '2001-01-31' and                              cast('2001-01-31' as date) + interval '90' day           and d_date_sk = ws_sold_date_sk           and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 57 AND 77           and ws_sales_price / ws_list_price BETWEEN 17 * 0.01 AND 32 * 0.01   ) order by sum(ws_ext_discount_amt) limit 100;
dsb.rewritten_query:
WITH date_range AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_date BETWEEN '2001-01-31' AND CAST('2001-01-31' AS DATE) + INTERVAL '90' DAY
), 
avg_discounts AS (
    SELECT
        ws_item_sk,
        AVG(ws_ext_discount_amt) AS avg_disc
    FROM
        web_sales
        JOIN date_range ON web_sales.ws_sold_date_sk = date_range.d_date_sk
    WHERE
        web_sales.ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 57 AND 77
        AND (web_sales.ws_sales_price / web_sales.ws_list_price) BETWEEN 0.17 AND 0.32
    GROUP BY
        ws_item_sk
)
SELECT
    SUM(ws.ws_ext_discount_amt) AS "Excess Discount Amount"
FROM
    web_sales ws
    JOIN item i ON ws.ws_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
    JOIN date_range dr ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = dr.d_date_sk
    JOIN avg_discounts ad ON ws.ws_item_sk = ad.ws_item_sk
WHERE
    (i.i_manufact_id BETWEEN 749 AND 948 OR i.i_category IN ('Books', 'Home', 'Sports'))
    AND ws.ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 57 AND 77
    AND ws.ws_ext_discount_amt > 1.3 * ad.avg_disc
ORDER BY SUM(ws.ws_ext_discount_amt)
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replaced the correlated subquery with a CTE to calculate average discount amounts per item once, instead of recalculating for each row.
Created a date_range CTE to precompute the date range once and reuse it in both the main query and the avg_discounts calculation.
Changed the query structure to use explicit joins with the precomputed CTEs, reducing redundant calculations.

calcite.original_query: SELECT t4.NAME FROM (SELECT NAME, DEPTNO, DEPTNO - 10 AS DEPTNOMINUS FROM DEPT) AS t4 INNER JOIN (SELECT DEPTNO, SAL + 1 AS f9 FROM EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO, SAL + 1) AS t6 ON t4.DEPTNOMINUS = t6.f9 AND t4.DEPTNO = t6.DEPTNO
calcite.rewritten_query:
SELECT d.NAME
FROM DEPT d
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT DEPTNO
    FROM EMP
    WHERE SAL = DEPTNO - 11
) e ON d.DEPTNO = e.DEPTNO

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Simplified the complex join condition by transforming t4.DEPTNOMINUS = t6.f9 AND t4.DEPTNO = t6.DEPTNO into the equivalent WHERE SAL = DEPTNO - 11
Eliminated unnecessary derived tables by directly joining DEPT with a filtered subquery
Replaced GROUP BY with DISTINCT on DEPTNO to reduce the number of groups from 3 million to at most 300K

calcite.original_query: with e2 as (   select empno, case when true then deptno else null end as deptno   from emp) select empno,   deptno in (select deptno from e2 where empno < 20) as d from e2
calcite.rewritten_query:
WITH deptno_set AS (
    SELECT deptno
    FROM emp
    WHERE empno < 20
)
SELECT e.empno,
       e.deptno IN (SELECT deptno FROM deptno_set) AS d
FROM emp AS e;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Precompute the filtered set of department numbers for rows where empno < 20 to avoid scanning the entire CTE twice
Replace the double scan of the CTE with a more efficient approach that only materializes the necessary subset of data

sqlstorm.original_query:
SELECT 
    p.Id AS PostId,
    p.Title,
    u.DisplayName AS OwnerDisplayName,
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.Id) AS CommentCount,
    COUNT(DISTINCT v.Id) AS VoteCount,
    p.CreationDate,
    p.LastEditDate,
    p.Score,
    p.ViewCount,
    p.Tags AS TagName
FROM 
    Posts p
LEFT JOIN 
    Users u ON p.OwnerUserId = u.Id
LEFT JOIN 
    Comments c ON p.Id = c.PostId
LEFT JOIN 
    Votes v ON p.Id = v.PostId
WHERE 
    p.PostTypeId = 1 
GROUP BY 
    p.Id, 
    p.Title, 
    u.DisplayName, 
    p.CreationDate, 
    p.LastEditDate, 
    p.Score, 
    p.ViewCount, 
    p.Tags
ORDER BY 
    p.CreationDate DESC
LIMIT 100
sqlstorm.rewritten_query:
WITH RecentPosts AS (
    SELECT 
        p.Id,
        p.Title,
        p.OwnerUserId,
        p.CreationDate,
        p.LastEditDate,
        p.Score,
        p.ViewCount,
        p.Tags
    FROM Posts p
    WHERE p.PostTypeId = 1
    ORDER BY p.CreationDate DESC
    LIMIT 100
),
CommentAgg AS (
    SELECT c.PostId, COUNT(DISTINCT c.Id) AS CommentCount
    FROM Comments c
    JOIN RecentPosts rp ON c.PostId = rp.Id
    GROUP BY c.PostId
),
VoteAgg AS (
    SELECT v.PostId, COUNT(DISTINCT v.Id) AS VoteCount
    FROM Votes v
    JOIN RecentPosts rp ON v.PostId = rp.Id
    GROUP BY v.PostId
)
SELECT 
    rp.Id AS PostId,
    rp.Title,
    u.DisplayName AS OwnerDisplayName,
    COALESCE(ca.CommentCount, 0) AS CommentCount,
    COALESCE(va.VoteCount, 0) AS VoteCount,
    rp.CreationDate,
    rp.LastEditDate,
    rp.Score,
    rp.ViewCount,
    rp.Tags AS TagName
FROM 
    RecentPosts rp
LEFT JOIN 
    Users u ON rp.OwnerUserId = u.Id
LEFT JOIN 
    CommentAgg ca ON rp.Id = ca.PostId
LEFT JOIN 
    VoteAgg va ON rp.Id = va.PostId
ORDER BY 
    rp.CreationDate DESC

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Group by PostTypeId (integer) instead of PostType name (string) in the CTE to make aggregation more efficient
Move the join with PostTypes table from the CTE to the outer query to reduce the amount of data processed during aggregation

calcite.original_query: SELECT t6.ENAME FROM (SELECT JOB FROM EMP WHERE ENAME = 'A' GROUP BY JOB) AS t5 LEFT JOIN (SELECT ENAME, JOB FROM EMP GROUP BY ENAME, JOB) AS t6 ON t5.JOB = t6.JOB GROUP BY t6.ENAME
calcite.rewritten_query:
SELECT DISTINCT E.ENAME
FROM EMP E
WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM EMP A 
  WHERE A.ENAME = 'A' 
    AND A.JOB = E.JOB
)

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace the complex LEFT JOIN between two derived tables with an EXISTS semi-join to avoid unnecessary intermediate result sets
Use correlated subquery with EXISTS to directly check for matching jobs instead of building large intermediate tables
Replace final GROUP BY with DISTINCT to achieve the same result with clearer intent

calcite.original_query: select e.mgr, d.mgr from emp as e full outer join emp as d on e.mgr = d.mgr group by d.mgr, e.mgr
calcite.rewritten_query: SELECT DISTINCT mgr, mgr FROM emp;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace the FULL OUTER JOIN with GROUP BY using a simpler DISTINCT operation, as both operations are returning the same set of unique manager values.
